title: [v4.9-rhel] Fix: Ensure HealthCheck exec session terminates on timeout
body:
Previously, the HealthCheck exec session would not terminate on timeout, allowing the healthcheck to run indefinitely.
Because Docker does that, Podman should kill the HealthCheck when it reaches the timeout.

In order to backport this patch, you need to backport this patch as well (It is included in this PR):
- https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/25003 

This Backport contains: 
- https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/25003
- https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/26086 [commit](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/26086/commits/499ea1168bd52a36e98927222a987a6b959f0b50)
- https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/26086 [commit](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/26086/commits/077649f9d0c53583654a61c94ebca0f4fa15b877)

> Comparing https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/499ea1168bd52a36e98927222a987a6b959f0b50#diff-bf52e0a2dfcea051750fa86d05c8a2ce247c66a92a8562570f4d3555d76664a6R872
this line`session.PIDData = getPidData(pid)` is removed because we would have to backport this PR #25906.

Fixes: https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-96914

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
Fixed a bug where healthchecks exceeding their timeout were not properly terminated; they now receive SIGTERM then SIGKILL, aligning with Docker and preventing indefinite execution.
The HelathCheck is executed without writing to the database. 
```

comments.body:
Logically speaking none of these changes has yet seen any significant uses, the run without hc without db one will just be released now in 5.5 and the other two are not even in 5.5. As such it is hard to tell what the consequences of these changes are and if it they might not cause other regressions. @mheon How do you feel about these backports?



And if we backport it here I assume it also means it mus be backported to all version up until 4.9, i.e. 5.2-rhel, 5.4-rhel as otherwise on upgrades the new feature/bug would be missing and thus cause regressions. 

cc @TomSweeneyRedHat
If we can get good QE testing I think it ought to be fine, but we will want to do a pretty comprehensive pass over healthchecks to verify the no DB writes bit.
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.

For auditing reasons, this is based on:
Run HealthCheck without saving the ExecSession to the database #25003
Fix: Ensure HealthCheck exec session terminates on timeout #26086 [commit](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/26086/commits/499ea1168bd52a36e98927222a987a6b959f0b50)
Fix: Ensure HealthCheck exec session terminates on timeout #26086 [commit](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/26086/commits/077649f9d0c53583654a61c94ebca0f4fa15b877)

LGTM
One quesiton that I'd like answered before merge
